chapter: Chapter: Parliament and the Making of Laws

questions.question: Which institution represents the will of the people at the national level in India?
questions.explanation: Parliament represents the people of India and makes laws on their behalf.

questions.question: Which of the following is a key function of Parliament in a democracy?
questions.explanation: Parliament checks the work of the government through debates and questioning.

questions.question: Which house of Parliament has the authority to introduce Money Bills?
questions.explanation: Money Bills can only be introduced in the Lok Sabha.

questions.question: Which institution ensures that the executive remains accountable to the legislature?
questions.explanation: The executive is responsible to Parliament in a parliamentary system.

questions.question: Which parliamentary procedure allows MPs to question ministers about government actions?
questions.explanation: During Question Hour MPs ask ministers questions regarding government policies.

questions.question: Which group in Parliament plays a crucial role in criticizing government policies?
questions.explanation: Opposition parties question and criticize government policies.

questions.question: Which stage in the law-making process involves discussion and debate on a proposed bill?
questions.explanation: Bills are debated in Parliament before being passed as laws.

questions.question: Which democratic principle is reflected when citizens elect Members of Parliament?
questions.explanation: Elected representatives represent citizens in Parliament.

questions.question: Which house of Parliament is considered the permanent house because it cannot be dissolved?
questions.explanation: Rajya Sabha is a permanent house and is not dissolved.

questions.question: Which institution approves bills after they are passed by both houses of Parliament?
questions.explanation: A bill becomes law only after the President gives assent.

questions.question: Which feature of Parliament ensures public discussion of national issues?
questions.explanation: Debates allow MPs to discuss important national issues.

questions.question: Which principle ensures that the government must maintain the support of the Lok Sabha?
questions.explanation: In parliamentary democracy the government must have the confidence of the Lok Sabha.

questions.question: Which body introduces most government bills in Parliament?
questions.explanation: Government ministers from the executive introduce most bills.

questions.question: Which process ensures discussion and modification of proposed laws?
questions.explanation: Debates allow MPs to suggest changes before passing laws.

questions.question: Which democratic institution ensures that government policies are publicly discussed?
questions.explanation: Parliament provides a forum for public discussion through debates.

questions.question: Which function of Parliament involves reviewing government spending?
questions.explanation: Parliament reviews and approves government budgets.

questions.question: Which parliamentary practice allows MPs to raise urgent public issues?
questions.explanation: Zero Hour allows MPs to raise urgent issues without prior notice.

questions.question: Which democratic principle ensures that government leaders are chosen by elected representatives?
questions.explanation: In a parliamentary system the government is formed by elected representatives.

questions.question: Which institution acts as a forum for national debate and policy discussion?
questions.explanation: Parliament provides a platform for national debate.

questions.question: Which feature ensures transparency in government functioning?
questions.explanation: Questioning ministers ensures transparency and accountability.

questions.question: Which institution ensures citizens’ representation at the national legislative level?
questions.explanation: Parliament represents the people and passes laws.

questions.question: Which parliamentary feature allows scrutiny of government policies?
questions.explanation: Debates and questioning help MPs evaluate government policies.

questions.question: Which democratic institution represents citizens through elected representatives?
questions.explanation: Parliament represents citizens through elected MPs.

questions.question: Which step is essential before a bill becomes a law?
questions.explanation: Bills must be debated and approved before becoming laws.

questions.question: Which democratic value is strengthened when Parliament debates national issues openly?
questions.explanation: Open debates promote transparency in governance.
